observations: SCOPE OF THIS ARTIFACT: this is a DELTA CONFIRMATION, not a fresh full re-review. .agent/qa/038-contract-review-rev1.json (revision_reviewed=1, approved=true) already independently re-verified all 26 verification entries, the loose-vs-strict operator_approval fix, and the guard entries against feature 034's real landed implementation. This addendum's sole job is to confirm nothing operative drifted between the revision-1 contract that review approved and the current, live revision-2 contract (post validator-Mode-A rejection-and-fix), per the dual-gate rule.

STRUCTURAL DIFF, PROGRAMMATIC, AGAINST THE EXACT REVISION-1 TEXT THIS EVALUATOR PREVIOUSLY REVIEWED (reconstructed verbatim from this evaluator's own round-2 review transcript, not re-derived from the generator's or coordinator's characterization of it): `contract_id`, `feature_id`, `name`, `status`, `spec_ref`, `scope` (both `files_create` -- all 7 entries, schema + 6 fixtures -- and `files_modify` -- NIZAM.json, schema/README.md), `estimated_lines` (231), `evidence_dir`, and `evidence_convention` are all byte-identical, Python dict/list equality confirmed. All six `design_notes` sub-fields (`nature_of_this_feature`, `schema_design`, `dry_run_proof`, `concurrency_fence`, `run_state_and_immutable_artifacts`, `rev1_summary`) are byte-identical -- no keys added, removed, or renamed.

VERIFICATION ARRAY: parsed both the reconstructed revision-1 array and the live revision-2 array; both have exactly 26 entries; a position-by-position equality scan (`acceptance_test` and `command` fields together) found ZERO differing entries. Every one of the 26 verification commands this evaluator independently re-executed and probed in the round-2 review (.agent/qa/038-contract-review-rev1.json) is therefore still the exact command running under revision 2 -- nothing was silently altered, renumbered, added, or removed under cover of the Mode-A fix.

NON_GOALS ARRAY: 12 entries in both revisions; exactly ONE differs -- non_goals[3] (0-indexed), changed from '...besides the five new tools/fixtures/preflight_verdict_*.json fixtures...' to '...besides the six new tools/fixtures/preflight_verdict_*.json fixtures...'. This is precisely the internal-consistency token Validator Mode A is reported to have flagged: revision 1 added a sixth fixture (tools/fixtures/preflight_verdict_invalid_approval_incomplete.json, confirmed already present in `scope.files_create` and already wired into verification entry 11's three-named-fixtures list and entry 26's scope-guard allow-list in BOTH revision 1 and revision 2, since neither of those was touched) but left this one non_goals sentence's stale count at 'five'. The fix brings the prose in line with a count every operative mechanism (files_create length, entry 11's fixture list, entry 26's allow-list) already agreed on -- it does not change what is authorized or verified, only a narrative count that was already lagging reality.

APPROVALS METADATA: `approvals.revisions` incremented 1 -> 2; a new `approvals.round1_mode_a_rejection` string field was added, recording the Mode-A rejection's content and explicitly noting (per the Durable State Rule) that no corresponding `.agent/validator/038-mode-a*.json` artifact was found on disk at the time of this review (`.agent/validator/`'s most recent entries remain 026-030, independently re-confirmed by this evaluator via `ls .agent/validator/`). This is bookkeeping/provenance only -- it does not alter scope, non_goals substance, or any verification command.

INDEPENDENT RE-CONFIRMATION, LIGHT-TOUCH (no fresh full re-execution needed since zero verification commands changed, per house convention for a metadata-only delta): re-ran `python3 -c "jsonschema.validate(...)"` against the live revision-2 contract and schema/contract.schema.json -- exits 0, schema-valid. Re-ran `bash tools/validate.sh` -- 'SUMMARY: 11 passed, 0 failed', unchanged from the state at the round-2 (revision-1) review. `git status --porcelain --untracked-files=all` shows only this feature's own untracked artifacts (.agent/contracts/038.json, .agent/qa/038-contract-review.json, .agent/qa/038-contract-review-rev1.json, this file, and the pre-existing v0.6.0-release-notes.md) plus nothing else; `git diff HEAD --stat` is empty. HEAD remains at 8534a77 (feature 034's landed commit), unchanged since the round-2 review.

No scratch-schema re-probing of the loose-vs-strict operator_approval gap was necessary in this delta pass: the schema_design and all 26 verification commands (including entries 7/8, the direct proofs of that fix) are byte-identical to the revision-1 text this evaluator already probed with disposable scratch schemas and confirmed sound in .agent/qa/038-contract-review-rev1.json. Re-running that probe against unchanged command text would add no new information; it is not repeated here to avoid redundant, non-load-bearing work, consistent with 'nothing operative drifted' being the finding of this delta.

REPOSITORY LEFT UNMODIFIED BY THIS DELTA REVIEW: no probe of any kind was run against a real repo path in this pass beyond read-only `git status`/`git diff`/`bash tools/validate.sh`/schema-validation invocations already exercised identically in the round-2 review; no scratch fixture was needed. `git status --porcelain --untracked-files=all` before and after this delta pass is unchanged except for this artifact's own addition.

final_verdict.summary: APPROVED at revision 2 (delta confirmation carrying forward the round-2 approval of revision 1, per .agent/qa/038-contract-review-rev1.json). A full programmatic structural diff against the exact revision-1 text this evaluator independently re-verified confirms the ONLY two changes are: (1) non_goals[3]'s stale fixture-count token corrected from 'five' to 'six' -- a pure prose fix aligning that one sentence with what files_create (7 entries: schema + 6 fixtures), verification entry 11 (three named negative fixtures including the sixth/incomplete-approval one), and verification entry 26 (scope-guard allow-list, 6 fixture paths) already agreed on in revision 1, changing nothing about what is authorized or verified; and (2) the `approvals` block's bookkeeping (revisions 1->2, plus a new `round1_mode_a_rejection` provenance field recording Mode A's rejection content and the absence of a corresponding .agent/validator/038-mode-a*.json artifact on disk). All 26 verification entries, all six design_notes sub-fields, scope.files_create/files_modify, estimated_lines, evidence_dir, and evidence_convention are byte-identical to the revision-1 text this evaluator already independently re-executed, probed with disposable loose-vs-strict scratch schemas, and approved. Re-confirmed live: the revision-2 contract is schema-valid against schema/contract.schema.json, and `bash tools/validate.sh` still reports 'SUMMARY: 11 passed, 0 failed'. Nothing operative drifted. The round-2 verdict (approved=true, fit for implementation as-is) carries forward unchanged to revision 2.
